nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, clear-water coral reefs and rocky substrates at depths of approximately 0–30 m; larvae occasionally drift north as far as Massachusetts and Nova Scotia during summer

diet.notes: In the wild, this species has been documented consuming polychaete worms, fan and spaghetti worms, fish eggs, small crustaceans, and the polyps of hard and soft corals. In captivity it is often described as a finicky feeder, particularly on arrival; vitamin-enriched live brine shrimp, chopped clam, squid, and shrimp have been reported as useful starter foods, transitioning to mysis shrimp and frozen carnivore preparations once the fish is feeding. Small meals offered at least three times daily are generally recommended.

summary: A peaceful Western Atlantic and Caribbean butterflyfish with a white body, yellow fins, and a distinctive dark spot on the rear dorsal fin. Generally not considered reef-safe and has been reported to be a finicky eater on arrival.
careNotes:
The Spotfin Butterflyfish (also sold as Spotfin Reef Butterfly) is a Western Atlantic species ranging from Florida and the Gulf of Mexico south to Brazil, with juveniles occasionally drifting as far north as Massachusetts and Nova Scotia in late summer. It is among the more approachable Caribbean butterflyfishes for the hobby but is still generally regarded as intermediate-level due to its finicky feeding habits on arrival and its tendency to nip sessile invertebrates.

A mature, well-established aquarium with live rock, open swimming space, and hiding places between the rockwork is generally recommended before introduction. The species is most active during the day, tends to use all swim zones, and has been observed to develop dusky cross-bands at night. Curious behavioral notes from retailer sources include occasional inverted swimming and the habit of squirting water above the surface in a fountain-like manner.

The species has been documented forming pairs during breeding in the wild, and aquarium pairs (introduced simultaneously) can usually be kept successfully; lone fish from the same species added separately have been reported to quarrel. It is not considered reef-safe — it has been observed to nip stony and soft coral polyps, tubeworms, and giant clams.

Cross-source disagreements:
- Tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 125 US gal (~473 L), reefapp.net lists 500 L, and saltwaterfish.com lists 100 US gal (~379 L). Mean ≈ 451 L; biased toward 500 L given prose cues about ample swimming space and active behaviour.
- Temperature: LiveAquaria 22.2–25.6 °C, FishBase 23.9–28.2 °C, saltwaterfish.com 23.3–25.6 °C. Averaged range 23–27 °C used here.
- Adult size: LiveAquaria states 8 in (~20 cm) and FishBase confirms 20 cm TL; range 15–20 cm captures typical adult to maximum length.
- Care level: LiveAquaria 'Moderate' and saltwaterfish.com 'Easy' versus prose hints of finicky feeding from multiple sources; intermediate selected as the central rating.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to form pairs during breeding in the wild and is oviparous. No reliable captive breeding has been documented for this species under typical hobby conditions.

additional.name: fishbase.mnhn.fr
additional.notes: Mirror returned HTTP 403; cross-checked the same species page via the fishbase.se mirror instead. Confirmed scientific name (Chaetodon ocellatus Bloch, 1787), family Chaetodontidae, order Acanthuriformes, maximum length 20 cm TL, depth range 0–30 m, Western Atlantic distribution (Florida to Brazil), temperature 23.9–28.2 °C (mean 27.2 °C), oviparous and pair-forming reproduction, IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: FishBase (mirror swap)
additional.notes: Used in place of the mnhn.fr mirror after a 403 there. Same FishBase record; values quoted above.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Additional cross-check: max length 20 cm, minimum tank volume 500 L, mostly peaceful temperament, not reef-safe (nips tubeworms, clams, soft/stony coral polyps), pairs possible if introduced together.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Additional cross-check: temperature 23.3–25.6 °C (74–78 °F), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.023–1.025 SG, adult 6–8 in, captive lifespan 5–7 years, minimum tank 100 US gal (~379 L), not reef-safe.

fish.breedingNotes: Forms pairs during breeding in the wild and is oviparous; captive reproduction has not been reliably documented under typical hobby conditions.
